page: Amazon Web Services m8a.8xlarge is a general-purpose virtual machine powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features 32 dedicated physical cores and 32 vCPUs utilizing the x86_64 AMD EPYC 9R45 processor, which operates at a clock speed of 4.5 GHz. The CPU architecture includes 48 KB L1d, 1.0 MB L2, and 32 MB L3 cache per core, alongside support for advanced vector extensions such as AVX-512 and AVX_VNNI. The system is equipped with 128.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 6 GHz, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 15 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate strong compute and memory capabilities. The instance achieves top-tier performance in Geekbench single-core and multi-core tests, as well as stress-ng single-core CPU evaluations. Memory bandwidth tests place the DDR5 memory subsystem in the top 25% for both read and write operations. In application-specific workloads, the server registers top-tier results for static web serving throughput, PassMark database operations, and single-threaded gzip compression. For machine learning tasks, LLM inference speeds for small and medium models rank in the top 10% and top 25% respectively, though performance drops to average when processing larger 70B models.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the m8a.8xlarge offers a balanced resource density suitable for general-purpose enterprise workloads without the premium cost of specialized memory-optimized or GPU-accelerated hardware. However, the lack of local NVMe SSD storage represents a notable tradeoff, requiring external block storage for data-intensive applications. Given its high-frequency AMD EPYC processor and robust memory bandwidth, this instance is suited for database operations, web serving, software compilation, and moderate-scale language model inference.
description: Amazon Web Services m8a.8xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 32 dedicated physical cores powered by an AMD EPYC 9R45 processor running at 4.5 GHz. It is configured with 128.0 GB of high-speed 6 GHz DDR5 memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core, but lacks local storage and dedicated GPU hardware. Operating on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, the instance delivers top-tier performance in single-core and multi-core Geekbench benchmarks, database operations, and static web serving. It also exhibits strong memory bandwidth and efficient text processing capabilities. While LLM inference for small and medium models is highly efficient, performance drops to average for large 70B models. Lacking local NVMe storage, it relies on network-attached storage. This instance is suited for enterprise web serving, database management, software compilation, and general-purpose computational workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m8a.8xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 32 dedicated AMD EPYC cores, 128 GB of DDR5 RAM, and 15 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering top-tier database and web serving performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m8a.8xlarge is a general-purpose instance with 32 AMD EPYC cores, 128 GB DDR5 RAM, and top-tier single-core CPU performance.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose platform featuring high-frequency AMD EPYC processors and DDR5 memory for database operations and web serving.
bullet_points: 32 dedicated AMD EPYC 9R45 physical cores operating at 4.5 GHz
128 GB of high-speed 6 GHz DDR5 memory with 4 GB per core
Top-tier performance in database operations and static web serving
15 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with Nitro hypervisor integration
No local storage or GPU accelerators included
Suited for enterprise databases, web servers, and software compilation
